verses.1: I am the man who has seen misery Because of the rod of His wrath.
verses.2: He has driven me and made me walk In darkness and not in light.
verses.3: Indeed, He has turned His hand against me Repeatedly all the day.
verses.4: He has consumed my flesh and my skin, He has broken my bones.
verses.5: He has besieged and surrounded me with bitterness and hardship.
verses.6: He has made me live in dark places, Like those who have long been dead.
verses.7: He has walled me in so that I cannot go out; He has made my chain heavy.
verses.8: Even when I cry out and call for help, He shuts out my prayer.
verses.9: He has blocked my ways with cut stone; He has twisted my paths.
verses.10: He is to me like a bear lying in wait, Like a lion in secret places.
verses.11: He has made my ways deviate, and torn me to pieces; He has made me desolate.
verses.12: He bent His bow And took aim at me as a target for the arrow.
verses.13: He made the arrows of His quiver Enter my inward parts.
verses.14: I have become a laughingstock to all my people, Their song of ridicule all the day.
verses.15: He has filled me with bitterness, He has made me drink plenty of wormwood.
verses.16: He has also made my teeth grind with gravel; He has made me cower in the dust.
verses.17: My soul has been excluded from peace; I have forgotten happiness.
verses.18: So I say, “My strength has failed, And so has my hope from the LORD.”
verses.19: Remember my misery and my homelessness, the wormwood and bitterness.
verses.20: My soul certainly remembers, And is bent over within me.
verses.21: I recall this to my mind, Therefore I wait.
verses.22: The LORD’S acts of mercy indeed do not end, For His compassions do not fail.
verses.23: They are new every morning; Great is Your faithfulness.
verses.24: “The LORD is my portion,” says my soul, “Therefore I wait for Him.”
verses.25: The LORD is good to those who await Him, To the person who seeks Him.
verses.26: It is good that he waits silently For the salvation of the LORD.
verses.27: It is good for a man to bear The yoke in his youth.
verses.28: Let him sit alone and keep quiet, Since He has laid it on him.
verses.29: Let him put his mouth in the dust; Perhaps there is hope.
verses.30: Let him give his cheek to the one who is going to strike him; Let him be filled with shame.
verses.31: For the Lord will not reject forever,
verses.32: For if He causes grief, Then He will have compassion In proportion to His abundant mercy.
verses.33: For He does not afflict willingly Or grieve the sons of mankind.
verses.34: To crush under one’s feet All the prisoners of the land,
verses.35: To deprive a man of justice In the presence of the Most High,
verses.36: To defraud someone in his lawsuit— Of these things the Lord does not approve.
verses.37: Who is there who speaks and it comes to pass, Unless the Lord has commanded it?
verses.38: Is it not from the mouth of the Most High That both adversity and good proceed?
verses.39: Of what can any living mortal, or any man, Complain in view of his sins?
verses.40: Let’s examine and search out our ways, And let’s return to the LORD.
verses.41: We raise our heart and hands Toward God in heaven;
verses.42: We have done wrong and rebelled; You have not pardoned.
verses.43: You have covered Yourself with anger And pursued us; You have slain and have not spared.
verses.44: You have veiled Yourself with a cloud So that no prayer can pass through.
verses.45: You have made us mere refuse and rubbish In the midst of the peoples.
verses.46: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
verses.47: Panic and pitfall have come upon us, Devastation and destruction;
verses.48: My eyes run down with streams of water Because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
verses.49: My eyes flow unceasingly, Without stopping,
verses.50: Until the LORD looks down And sees from heaven.
verses.51: My eyes bring pain to my soul Because of all the daughters of my city.
verses.52: My enemies without reason Hunted me down like a bird;
verses.53: They have silenced me in the pit And have thrown stones on me.
verses.54: Waters flowed over my head; I said, “I am cut off!”
verses.55: I called on Your name, LORD, Out of the lowest pit.
verses.56: You have heard my voice, “Do not cover Your ear from my plea for relief, From my cry for help.”
verses.57: You came near on the day I called to You; You said, “Do not fear!”
verses.58: Lord, You have pleaded my soul’s cause; You have redeemed my life.
verses.59: LORD, You have seen my oppression; Judge my case.
verses.60: You have seen all their vengeance, All their schemes against me.
verses.61: You have heard their reproach, LORD, All their schemes against me.
verses.62: The lips of my assailants and their talk Are against me all day long.
verses.63: Look at their sitting and their rising; I am their mocking song.
verses.64: You will repay them, LORD, In accordance with the work of their hands.
verses.65: You will give them shamelessness of heart, Your curse will be on them.
verses.66: You will pursue them in anger and eliminate them From under the heavens of the LORD!
